front: Work: argues 'theology is anthropology' - the concept of God is an anthropomorphic projection of the mind; reverence for God is really reverence for Man the species
Work: draws on D.F. Strauss's The Life of Jesus Critically Examined; claims belief in revelation 'poisons' innate moral sense
Philosopher: Max Stirner responded to this thinker's most famous work in The Ego and Its Own; Bruno Bauer attacked in an essay on this thinker's 'Characteristics'
Philosopher: subject of Marx's 11 Theses, the last of which says 'Philosophers have only interpreted the world; the point is to change it'; criticized for restricting human essence to the individual
Philosopher: coined the punning dictum 'Man is what he eats' (a pun only in German: 'ist was er isst') from a review of Moleschott's book
Philosopher: declared the impossibility of a 'religion of the elephants,' arguing that self-consciousness dependent on nature defines mankind
